Atmos Momochi

A penthouse residence on Fukuoka's waterfront.
271 sqm. Top floor. A singular opportunity.

Enquire about this property

About This Residence

A Residence That Stands on Its Own.

ATMOS MOMOCHI is a 25-storey residential tower on the Momochihama waterfront in Fukuoka's Sawara Ward. This listing is the penthouse — 271.43 sqm of interior space with a 65 sqm roof balcony, overlooking the Hakata Bay coastline, Fukuoka Tower, and the city beyond.

Built in 1993 and fully renovated in August 2017, the residence combines generous proportions with a level of finish rarely found in Fukuoka's resale market. The building comprises 182 units. This is one of few opportunities to acquire the penthouse.

Properties of this scale and location do not appear frequently. This page exists to present the facts clearly for those who may be considering it.

Features

What Sets This Residence Apart

  • The penthouse, 25F — unobstructed views in multiple directions.
  • 271 sqm interior — a scale that is difficult to find in Fukuoka's condominium market.
  • 65 sqm roof balcony — private outdoor space at penthouse level.
  • Authentic washitsu with master-crafted joinery — a traditional Japanese room of genuine quality, rarely seen in a modern high-rise.
  • Curved panoramic windows in the master bedroom — sweeping night views across the city.
  • Marble flooring with underfloor heating in the living area — renovated to a high specification in 2017.
  • Two bathrooms, two washrooms, built-in air conditioning throughout.

Suitability

For a Considered Buyer

This is not an entry-level investment property. It may suit:

  • A buyer seeking a primary or secondary residence in Fukuoka with space, privacy, and a waterfront position.
  • A family or individual who values generous living area over compact efficiency.
  • Someone looking for a long-term hold in a location with limited comparable inventory.
  • An overseas buyer considering Fukuoka as a base — a sophisticated alternative to the density of Tokyo or Osaka.

It is less likely to suit those prioritising rental yield, proximity to Tenjin or Hakata Station, or newer construction.

Due Diligence

Points to Consider

  • The building was completed in 1993. Structural condition, repair history, and long-term maintenance plans should be reviewed.
  • Management and repair reserve fees total approximately ¥106,300 per month including parking. Ongoing costs should be factored into any holding calculation.
  • Fujisaki Station is a 16-minute walk. The location is waterfront-oriented, not city-centre-oriented.
  • Resale liquidity for properties at this price level in Fukuoka is limited. This is a market with fewer comparable transactions.
  • Foreign buyers should confirm financing eligibility, tax obligations, and ownership structure before proceeding.
